research_answer: While non-traditional agriculture tripled in per capita terms between 1979 and 2005, it remains too small to meaningfully reshape Peru's export structure. It contributed only 6.1% of post-1993 export growth and represented just 5.8% of the overall export basket in 2005 — tiny figures compared to the 78% of export recovery driven by traditional sectors like mining and hydrocarbons. The impressive growth rate is in part a function of starting from a very small base. So while the sector shows promise, it has not come close to offsetting Peru's continued dependence on capital-intensive primary commodities.
supporting_quotes: the emergence of the non-traditional agriculture sector, which we can see is more than three times as large in 2005 as it was in 1979 (in per capita terms). However, Figure 4 shows that the non-traditional agriculture sector was of minor importance in the export recovery post-1993, contributing only 6.1% of the export growth. In terms of the overall export basket in 2005, non-traditional agriculture only amounts to 5.8%.
78% of the recovery was also due to traditional products

data_points.metric: growth in non-traditional agriculture exports per capita
